content.claim: Composition of SNI growth is uneven: severe learning disorders increased 12% in 2021/2022 alone (45,300 children); boys are classified SNI at 11% versus girls at 6.1%; and county-level variation ranges from 5.4% to 14%, a 2.6-fold spread far larger than plausible true prevalence differences.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Large regional variation suggests administrative/diagnostic practice differences but this is inference, not confirmed by sources
Sex disparity may reflect biological, diagnostic bias, or referral-pathway differences; none is established here
No independent audit of whether regional definitions are applied consistently
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
